comment.body:
Alma, this might be a really stupid and simple question but I’ve genuinely been thinking about this recently: What exactly moves the prices of SPX and VIX since they’re just indices that aren’t tradable? 

Is it the buys and sell of the options that drive the spot price? Is it the buys and sells of the spot stocks that make up the index and each weighting of said stock is the applied to it’s overall movement? If so are those stocks driven by the buying/selling of spot or the derivatives themselves? 

I ask because it almost seems like options markets drive spot price in stocks and indices more than the actual spot buying and selling of the equity does. But I’m not sure how that would really work (like how can the derivative drive the direction of the thing it’s derived from?)
comment.raw_body:

I ask because it almost seems like options markets drive spot price in stocks and indices more than the actual spot buying and selling of the equity does. But I’m not sure how that would really work (like how can the derivative drive the direction of the thing it’s derived from?)
